module_mood_contract: Joyful first creation — 'I made something that actually sounds like me.' The student discovers their own context is the differentiator; the guardrail is the control. No edit should make M1 feel like a tools-setup or prompt-engineering tutorial.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages the room. No authored conversation cues.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:21-23: '> **In-room cohort opening (self-study readers, skip to the question below).**\n>\n> Let’s hear from your sponsor first (the CEO / CTO / SVP who brought you here). They’re in the room with you, as a participant, not as a visitor. They’ve got one sentence in their own words: why they’re here, and what they don’t yet know. Something like *“I want to learn this with you, not delegate it.”* No corporate kickoff, no agenda. What matters isn’t the sentence. It’s that your sponsor said it, out loud, before anyone else. That’s the license the next seven modules run on.' — This is cohort-management prose in the student-facing body. It instructs the sponsor on what to say, tells the room what to register, and names a delivery precondition ('the license the next seven modules run on'). The self-study skip instruction is inside the blockquote. Trainer cohort-management of this kind belongs below <!-- maintainer -->, not in body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Move the sponsor-intro beat entirely below <!-- maintainer --> as a pre-session trainer note. The opener question ('where did the output come out generic?') already anchors the mood without the sponsor scaffold.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Hard-grep room-share commands banned from student-facing body.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:22: 'They’ve got one sentence in their own words... Something like *“I want to learn this with you, not delegate it.”*... What matters isn’t the sentence. It’s that your sponsor said it, **out loud**, before anyone else.' — The phrase 'out loud' is a room-share command within the student-facing body. The broader block instructs public vocalization by a named participant. This is the shape the hard-grep ban targets.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Same fix as pedagogy rule 27 — move sponsor-intro beat to maintainer block. 'Out loud' disappears with it.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The module has three content items in sequence: [Opening: Context is King] → [Exercise: Paint by agent with guardrails] → [Closing: Iterate and learn]. 'Context is King' reads as a ceiling-demo / arms-the-student opener (it introduces the vocabulary 'context' and 'guardrail' that the exercise then enacts), not a meta-frame lecture naming a pattern the student just lived through. The closer 'Iterate and learn' is structurally the meta-frame. Placement is consistent with the check_lectures rule: demo/vocab lecture opens, pattern-naming lecture closes.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Minimal lecturing before contrast-mood exercises.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The opener lecture (Context is King) precedes the exercise. M1 is a joy-creation module, not a contrast-mood module. The contrast-mood dosage rule applies primarily to M5-style modules. One short lecture before the exercise is within the dosage limit.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Both Debrief forcing functions ('Run a retro' and 'Spawn a subagent to give an unbiased read') are inside fenced blocks the student copies. Body prose between the prompts describes what happens and what to watch for — it does not encode the forcing function itself. Speed-runner reading prompt-to-prompt only: both moves land.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No placeholders inside fenced blocks.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Debrief prompt 1: no [BRACKETS]. References 'module-1/personal-brand-generation.md' as a deterministic path — correct. Debrief prompt 2: no placeholders. 'module-1/site.html' is a deterministic path.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Debrief prompt 1: lead-in is 'Ask Claude to retro your rules file.' (5 words, command verb). Debrief prompt 2: lead-in is 'Ask Claude to spawn a subagent to read the site cold.' (12 words, command verb).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Canonical presentation shape — lead-in, label with destination, blank line, fenced block.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Both Debrief prompts follow: lead-in sentence → '**Prompt** *(Claude Code)*' → blank line → fenced block. Shape is canonical.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Artifact destinations are explicit subpaths, never naked filenames (Agents 101-specific).
rules_evaluated.evidence: Debrief prompt 1: 'Read `module-1/personal-brand-generation.md`' and 'overwrite `module-1/personal-brand-generation.md`' — both use explicit subpaths. Debrief prompt 2: 'module-1/site.html' — explicit subpath. No naked filenames.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Markdown shout inside fenced prompt blocks is dead weight.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Debrief prompt 1: uses numbered items (1./2.) and plain text. No **bold** or *italic* inside the fence. Debrief prompt 2: plain text and numbered items inside fence. Clean.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named and honored.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Trace phase 1 mood_score: 8. Close mood_score: 8. Big Idea ('genuinely yours, not generic'), opener question ('where did the output come out generic?'), and Debrief structure (two-pass retro + cold-critic) all serve the joyful-creation mood contract. The module does not resolve M2's compounding mood prematurely — it plants the seed ('Module 2 compounds it') without delivering the system.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Epistemic mood is not-knowing, not having-done-wrong.
rules_evaluated.evidence: M1's mood is joyful creation, not unease. Rule 2 targets unease modules. N/A here, but checked: no 'having-done-wrong' framing found in the module.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with discipline, not failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea: 'With the right guardrails, you create output that’s genuinely yours, not generic.' — leads with the positive discipline (guardrails → personal output). 'Generic' appears as the negative case being addressed, not the title.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategic themes recur in Key Concepts.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Key Concepts: (1) 'Guardrails are structured context, not a checklist' — instances the context/compounding theme. (2) 'You are the world’s best evaluator of your own profile. Domain expertise IS the eval' — instances the self-knowledge/evaluator theme. (3) 'The guardrail IS the control: average output becomes great not because you prompted better' — instances the steering/control theme. (4) 'You made this. Module 2 compounds it.' — instances the compounding theme. Four concepts, four theme instances. Solid.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity check — no front-running downstream teaching moments.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The module introduces guardrails, context, and personal output quality. It does NOT introduce memory systems, agent files, scheduling, or multi-agent patterns — all owned by M2+. The one forward pointer ('Module 2 compounds it') is a hook, not a pre-delivery of M2's teaching moment.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Walk-away test (M2+). Does the student leave while the agent works?
rules_evaluated.evidence: Rule 1 explicitly scopes to M2+. M1 is the first module — no walk-away mechanic required or expected.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Between-module reading appears in two places (end of previous module + current Prework).
rules_evaluated.evidence: '## Homework after Module 1, before Module 2' section at line 86-88 links to [Module 2 prework](lectures/module-2-prework.md) with specific action items (connectors, plan-mode primer, Karpathy post, Lindenberg piece). Prework section for M1 itself lists prework for M1 (snake game, calendar connector, what-is-an-agent supplementary) — not M2 prework, which is correct: M1 prework is at M1's Prework section; M2 prework is linked in M1's Homework section. Symmetric duplication shape is honored.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Student agency — adults decide. No forced pauses or yoga-instructor pacing.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No forced pauses in the module body. The Debrief's 'Two patterns to watch' and 'Then, Claude as cold critic' paragraphs describe what to observe, not when to stop. No compulsion language found.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Exercise quality ladder — target 8, design 9 for peaks.
rules_evaluated.evidence: M1 is the joy-module — a designated 9-design peak per the quality ladder rule ('M1 joy, M5 rescue, M6 leverage, M8 awe'). Trace: mood 8, is_me_rating 8. The two-pass retro + cold-critic Debrief structure is a genuine wow-moment design (student surprised by both their own rules file AND Claude's cold picks aligning with their gut). Functional 8 in the trace; designed for 9.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Key Concepts shape — theme breadcrumbs, not standalone section.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Key Concepts uses tactical bullets in module voice. Final bullet — 'You made this. Module 2 compounds it.' — functions as a breadcrumb hooking the compounding theme in module-native language. No standalone 'Themes this module instances:' section. Clean shape.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc: close hands off to next module's open.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Next section: 'You just made great output AND packaged what you learned into a file the agent can re-read next time. That’s the move that compounds. Module 2 takes the same idea and systematises it (the memory, the agents, the room rules) so every future module’s work builds on every previous one.' — names exactly what M2 opens with (memory, agents, room rules). Handoff is explicit and correctly pitched: M1 lands 'compound interest on a rule file'; M2 systematises it.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Self-report inflation — debrief prompts carry residual med-risk by design.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Debrief prompt 1 contains self-audit-charity mitigations: 'Find what’s still surface; don’t defend the file as-is.' and 'Sort each rule into keep, sharpen, or drop.' Body prose after the prompt names the self-report risk: 'The LLM is generous to a rules file it just helped write... The prose summary rounds corners.' This matches the compounded rule that debrief prompts carry residual med-risk by design — the callout is the mitigation. The risk is named and present; it is not a blocker for M1 which is the first instance of this pattern.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Specify which CLAUDE.md layer — every time.
rules_evaluated.evidence: M1 deliberately creates NO CLAUDE.md (per maintainer block: 'Module 1 deliberately creates no `CLAUDE.md`.'). No CLAUDE.md layer reference in student-facing body — correct, since no write happens this module. N/A for the layer-specification rule.
